nodes.name: Manual Trigger
nodes.notes: Operator clicks 'Execute Workflow' in n8n UI to fire a brief on demand. Equivalent to SSHing in and running `python3 morning_brief_generator.py`.

nodes.name: Webhook Trigger
nodes.notes: Webhook POST /webhook/morning-brief. Body: {reason: 'sms' | 'incident' | 'manual'}. HORIZON's SMS handler hits this with reason=sms when operator texts 'BRIEF'. Grafana alertmanager hits this with reason=incident.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger (every 5m)
nodes.notes: Fires every 5 minutes. The Python script's --check-schedule flag reads operator_config.brief_schedule and only proceeds when local-time matches the configured window. Default state: brief_schedule=NULL → exits silently.

nodes.name: Run Brief (webhook)
nodes.notes: Operator must adjust the absolute path if scripts live elsewhere on LA. systemd path: /opt/bhn/scripts/horizon/ (mirror of repo). The script handles trigger reasons internally via --reason and --on-incident.

nodes.name: Run Brief (schedule-check)
nodes.notes: Runs every 5 minutes. The Python script self-gates against operator_config.brief_schedule + last_brief_sent_at. Exits silently when not due — no email, no notification.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: $('Run Brief (webhook)').first().json.exitCode === 0 ? 'sent' : 'failed', reason: $('Parse Webhook Reason').first().json.reason, stdout: ($('Run Brief (webhook)').first().json.stdout || '').slice(0, 500), stderr: ($('Run Brief (webhook)').first().json.stderr || '').slice(0, 500) }) }}
